## [00:00 - 03:01]

So in this lesson students will learn the overview of supply chain operation and in my view there are top three things on the supply chain operation. The first one is sourcing, the second bit is production and the last one is logistics. It's very important because this is where you're actually going to scale your business because from the first day till now you're probably working with third party suppliers, you're working with selling other people's products and you want to produce your own brand, you want to produce your own products, your own packaging and so that you can control your own margin and pricing. So it's very important to understand each part of this supply chain operation to really scale your business. So in sourcing that consists obviously you know how to find the right suppliers and how to set up the relationship and how do you give your design, your product to the manufacturer and so that they can give you the right quote and also production. When you put through an order to your supplier and that goes into the manufacturing process, depending on what product that is usually it takes anywhere between 15 days and 90 days and so there's a lot of things going involved in that process for example giving the supplier the order and locking your production slot and understanding where they get all the raw material ready and also when you're going to produce a product. So it's important because you want to make sure that they're following your instructions, it's important because you want to make sure they're producing the product at your quality standard and at certain stage you want to be able to visit them and making sure they're doing the right things. Logistics is a very, very important part of the process because you know you have a great product but it means absolutely nothing if you can't deliver to your end consumer. So there are three big parts in under logistics. The first one is inward freight and usually we use freight forwarding and which means that from origin to your destination warehouse. So it could be China, it could be Japan, it could be Vietnam. So you ship the container all the way to your destination. So let's just say I deliver the container from China to Melbourne and I've got a warehouse here and the second part is warehousing. So either you're working with an external 3PL partner or you do warehousing yourself and the last part of this logistics I call it last mile delivery. As you as order come in, you ship out the orders from your warehouse or 3PL. The action item for this lesson is to

## [03:01 - 03:08]

download the supply chain flow chart and see if that are fitting your business model and I'll see you in the next lesson.
